Technical Reference Manual

Table of Contents

  1. Introduction
    1. Purpose
    2. Scope
  2. Installation
    1. Prerequisites
    2. Setup Steps
  3. Configuration
  4. Usage
  5. Troubleshooting

1. Introduction

This document provides comprehensive technical guidance for the software system. It covers installation, configuration, and usage in sufficient detail to allow administrators and developers to deploy and operate the system effectively.

1.1 Purpose

The purpose of this manual is to serve as a definitive reference for all aspects of the system. It supplements quick-start guides and online documentation with detailed specifications and procedures.

1.2 Scope

This manual applies to version 3.0 and later releases. References to earlier versions are explicitly noted where behaviour has changed.

2. Installation

The installation process is straightforward on all supported platforms. Follow the steps in this chapter carefully to avoid common configuration errors.

2.1 Prerequisites

Before installing, ensure that your system meets the minimum requirements: a 64-bit operating system, at least 4 GB of RAM, and 10 GB of available disk space.

2.2 Setup Steps

Download the installer from the official distribution channel. Run the installer as an administrator and follow the on-screen prompts to complete the installation.

3. Configuration

After installation, open the configuration file located in the config/ directory. The file uses TOML syntax and is self-documented with inline comments.

4. Usage

Start the application from the command line or using the provided system service. Refer to the command-line reference for a full list of available options and flags.

5. Troubleshooting

If you encounter problems, consult the log files in logs/ and compare against the known issues list in Appendix A. Most common issues are resolved by verifying configuration values and restarting the service.
